description: HybridSec Agent — Rule definitions for vulnerability detection. Each rule maps a vulnerability type to its base CVSS score, exploit status, and fix template. Used by Module 1 (detection) and Module 3 (rule-based scoring).

rules.title: SSH Root Login Enabled
rules.description: The SSH server permits direct root login. An attacker who obtains the root password gains full system control without needing privilege escalation.

rules.title: SSH Password Authentication Enabled
rules.description: SSH allows password-based login, making the server vulnerable to brute-force and credential-stuffing attacks. Key-based authentication should be enforced.

rules.title: SSH Protocol Version 1 Enabled
rules.description: SSH Protocol v1 has known cryptographic weaknesses (weak MAC, no server authentication). All modern deployments must use Protocol 2.

rules.title: SSH Running on Default Port 22
rules.description: SSH is on the default port 22, which is targeted by automated bots and scanners 24/7. Moving to a non-standard port reduces noise significantly.

rules.title: SSH Permits Empty Passwords
rules.description: SSH is configured to allow login with empty passwords. Any account without a password is fully accessible via SSH.

rules.title: SSH X11 Forwarding Enabled
rules.description: X11 forwarding is enabled, which can be exploited to hijack graphical sessions or perform port forwarding attacks.

rules.title: Firewall Not Active
rules.description: No host-based firewall (UFW/iptables) is running. All ports are reachable from any IP without restriction.

rules.title: Firewall Default Policy Allows All Traffic
rules.description: The firewall default INPUT policy is ACCEPT. All ports are open unless explicitly denied. The policy should be DROP or REJECT with explicit allow rules.

rules.title: Non-Root User with UID 0
rules.description: A user account that is not 'root' has UID 0, giving it full superuser privileges. This is a backdoor-level finding.

rules.title: User Account with Empty Password
rules.description: One or more local user accounts have no password set. These accounts are accessible without any credentials.

rules.title: Weak System Password Policy
rules.description: The system PAM password policy does not enforce minimum length, complexity, or expiry. Users can set trivially guessable passwords.

rules.title: MySQL/MariaDB Exposed on Public Interface
rules.description: MySQL (port 3306) is bound to a public-facing interface. Remote attackers can attempt direct database connections or brute-force credentials.

rules.title: Unexpected SUID Binary Found
rules.description: A file with the SUID bit set was found outside of standard system locations (/bin, /sbin, /usr/bin, /usr/sbin). SUID files run as owner (often root) regardless of who executes them and are common privilege escalation vectors.

rules.title: SSL/TLS Certificate Expired
rules.description: The server's SSL certificate has passed its expiry date. Connections will display security warnings and may be rejected by browsers and clients.

rules.title: SSL/TLS Certificate Expiring Within 30 Days
rules.description: The server SSL certificate expires within 30 days. Renewal should be scheduled immediately to avoid service disruption.

rules.title: No SSL/TLS Certificate Configured
rules.description: No SSL certificate is installed. All web traffic is transmitted in plaintext and is vulnerable to interception.

rules.title: High Number of Failed Login Attempts Detected
rules.description: Unusually high failed authentication attempts in auth.log indicate an ongoing brute-force or credential-stuffing attack.

rules.title: Sensitive Service Port Exposed to Network
rules.description: A port for a sensitive internal service (database, admin panel, cache) is reachable from the network. These services should be firewalled.

rules.title: Software Package with Known CVE Installed
rules.description: An installed package has a known CVE vulnerability and a security update is available but not yet applied.

rules.title: Apache HTTP Server Outdated
rules.description: The installed Apache version is outdated and may contain known exploitable vulnerabilities. Update to the latest stable release.

rules.title: Nginx Web Server Outdated
rules.description: The installed Nginx version may contain known vulnerabilities. Update to the latest stable release.

rules.title: Low System Hardening Score (Lynis < 60)
rules.description: The Lynis system audit returned a hardening index below 60 out of 100, indicating significant security configuration deficiencies across the system.

rules.title: Critical Disk Usage (>90%)
rules.description: Disk usage exceeds 90%. Full disks prevent security log writes, can halt services, and may be used to cause denial of service.

rules.title: NTP Time Synchronization Not Configured
rules.description: System time is not synchronized via NTP. Inaccurate timestamps make security log correlation unreliable and can affect certificate validation.
